# {{< i18n stakeholder_register >}} {}

## Purpose of This Document

This Stakeholder Register identifies and classifies all key stakeholders
involved in, or impacted by, the **Subsea Benthic Analysis AI project**,
defining:

- Roles and responsibilities
- Level of interest and influence
- Engagement strategy
- Decision authority

This document is a **mandatory PMBOK artifact** and must be approved **before
finalization of the Project Management Plan (PMP)**.

---

## Stakeholder Classification Principles

Stakeholders are classified according to:

- **Influence**: ability to affect decisions or outcomes  
- **Interest**: level of concern with project results  
- **Authority**: decision or validation power  

Environmental and ecological stakeholders are treated as **primary
stakeholders**, regardless of organizational hierarchy.

---

## Stakeholder Register

| ID | Stakeholder | Organization / Unit | Role in Project | Interest | Influence | Primary Responsibility | Engagement Strategy |
|----|------------|---------------------|-----------------|----------|-----------|------------------------|--------------------|
| S1 | Project Sponsor | NOAA / SUB / Petrobras | Strategic Sponsor | High | High | Strategic direction, funding, final approvals | Executive reviews, Stage‑Gates |
| S2 | Environmental SMEs | NOAA / SUB | Scientific Authority | High | High | Ecological validation, acceptance authority | Continuous validation, reviews |
| S3 | Product Owner (PO) | NOAA / SUB | Business & Value Owner | High | High | OKR alignment, prioritization, value tracking | Weekly checkpoints |
| S4 | Project Manager | SUB | Delivery Authority | High | Medium | Planning, execution, reporting | Daily coordination |
| S5 | Data Science Lead | SUB / TIC | Technical Authority | Medium | Medium | Model design, performance decisions | Technical reviews |
| S6 | Data Engineering Lead | SUB / TIC | Pipeline & Data Authority | Medium | Medium | Data ingestion, versioning, infrastructure | Sprint alignment |
| S7 | IT / Infrastructure | Petrobras IT | Platform Support | Low | Medium | Compute, storage, security | Resource planning |
| S8 | Environmental Governance | SUB / Compliance | Oversight & Audit | Medium | High | Environmental compliance, auditability | Formal checkpoints |
| S9 | Operations / AUV Teams | SUB / Operations | Data Providers | Medium | Low | Image acquisition, metadata accuracy | On-demand alignment |
| S10 | Executive Management | Petrobras | Oversight | Low | High | Strategic visibility, escalation | Executive briefings |

---

## Stakeholder Influence vs Interest Matrix (Summary)

| Category | Stakeholders |
|--------|--------------|
| High Influence / High Interest | Sponsor, Environmental SMEs, PO |
| High Influence / Low Interest | Executive Management, Compliance |
| Low Influence / High Interest | Data Science, Data Engineering |
| Low Influence / Low Interest | Operations (AUV Teams) |

---

## Key Stakeholder Roles and Decision Rights

### Environmental SMEs (NOAA / SUB)
- Final authority on ecological validity
- Approval power at ecological Stage‑Gates (G1, G2)
- Veto right if ecological risk is identified

### Project Sponsor (NOAA / SUB)
- Go / No‑Go authority at all Stage‑Gates
- Final approval of scope, budget, and scaling

### Product Owner (PO)
- Owns [OKR at section @sec-okr]
- Validates business and environmental value delivery
- Prioritizes backlog based on ecological impact

---

## Stakeholder Risks and Mitigation

| Risk | Stakeholder | Mitigation |
|----|------------|------------|
| Ecological trust erosion | Environmental SMEs | Early and continuous involvement |
| Misalignment on value | Sponsor / PO | OKR and KPI transparency |
| Technical over-optimization | Data Science | Stage‑Gates with ecological KPIs |
| Adoption resistance | Operations | Early communication |

---

## Stakeholder Engagement Cadence (High-Level)

| Stakeholder Group | Engagement Type | Frequency |
|------------------|-----------------|-----------|
| Sponsor | Executive review | Monthly / Gates |
| Environmental SMEs | Validation workshops | Weekly / Bi-weekly |
| PO & PM | Alignment checkpoints | ≥ 2× per week |
| Technical Team | Delivery sync | Weekly |
| Compliance / Governance | Audit checkpoints | As required |

---

## Approval and Baseline

This Stakeholder Register is considered **baselined** upon approval and must be
updated via formal change control if stakeholder roles, influence, or authority
change.
